fix(format): expand ReadyIssue to match bd JSON schema

Added missing fields to ReadyIssue struct for bd parity:
- acceptance_criteria, assignee, description, estimated_minutes, notes, owner

Added description field to BlockedIssueOutput for bd parity.

Fixes beads_rust-4mq7. All 322 conformance tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"beads_rust-adr","title":"comments Command Group Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br comments` command group for managing issue comments. Comments provide a discussion thread for each issue, enabling collaboration and context preservation.

## CLI Interface
```
br comments <SUBCOMMAND>

Subcommands:
  add <issue-id> <text>     Add a comment to an issue
  list <issue-id>           List comments on an issue
  delete <comment-id>       Delete a comment
  edit <comment-id> <text>  Edit a comment (optional)

Options:
  --author <NAME>           Override comment author (defaults to git user)
  --json                    Output as JSON
  --robot                   Machine-readable output
```

## Technical Requirements

### Comment Model
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Comment {
    pub id: String,           // Unique comment ID (uuid or hash)
    pub issue_id: String,     // Parent issue
    pub content: String,      // Comment text (Markdown supported)
    pub author: String,       // Author name/email
    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,
}
```

### Storage Operations
```rust
impl SqliteStorage {
    pub fn add_comment(&mut self, comment: &Comment) -> Result<()> {
        // 1. Insert comment
        // 2. Write event (comment_added)
        // 3. Mark issue as dirty
        // 4. Update issue.updated_at
    }
    
    pub fn get_comments(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Comment>> {
        let sql = "SELECT * FROM comments WHERE issue_id = ? ORDER BY created_at ASC";
        // Return in chronological order
    }
    
    pub fn delete_comment(&mut self, comment_id: &str) -> Result<()> {
        // 1. Delete comment
        // 2. Write event (comment_deleted)
        // 3. Mark parent issue as dirty
    }
    
    pub fn update_comment(&mut self, comment_id: &str, content: &str) -> Result<()> {
        // 1. Update comment content and updated_at
        // 2. Write event (comment_updated)
        // 3. Mark parent issue as dirty
    }
}
```

### Comment ID Generation
Comments use separate ID space from issues:
```rust
fn generate_comment_id() -> String {
    // Option 1: UUID v4
    uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string()
    
    // Option 2: Timestamp + random suffix
    format!("c-{}-{}", timestamp_millis(), random_suffix(4))
}
```

### Author Resolution
```rust
fn resolve_author(explicit: Option<&str>) -> String {
    explicit.map(String::from)
        .or_else(|| git_user_name())
        .or_else(|| env::var("USER").ok())
        .unwrap_or_else(|| "anonymous".into())
}
```

## Output Formats

### comments list (human)
```
Comments on bd-abc12 (3 total):

[c-123] 2025-01-15 14:30 by alice@example.com
  This needs to handle edge cases where the input is empty.
  We should add validation in the parse_input function.

[c-124] 2025-01-15 15:45 by bob@example.com
  Good point. I will add that in the next commit.

[c-125] 2025-01-16 09:00 by alice@example.com
  Verified the fix works. LGTM.
```

### comments add (human)
```
✓ Added comment c-126 to bd-abc12
```

### JSON output
```json
{
  "comments": [
    {
      "id": "c-123",
      "issue_id": "bd-abc12",
      "content": "This needs to handle edge cases...",
      "author": "alice@example.com",
      "created_at": "2025-01-15T14:30:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 3
}
```

## JSONL Export Format
```jsonl
{"id":"c-123","issue_id":"bd-abc12","content":"...","author":"alice@example.com","created_at":"2025-01-15T14:30:00Z","updated_at":"2025-01-15T14:30:00Z"}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] add: Create comment with auto-generated ID
- [ ] add: Auto-detect author from git or env
- [ ] add: Mark parent issue dirty
- [ ] add: Write audit event
- [ ] list: Show all comments in chronological order
- [ ] list: Display author and timestamp
- [ ] delete: Remove comment by ID
- [ ] delete: Write audit event
- [ ] Export comments to comments.jsonl
- [ ] Import comments from comments.jsonl
- [ ] Human and JSON output formats

## Unit Tests
- Add comment creates record
- Comment ID is unique
- Author defaults to git user
- List returns chronological order
- Delete removes comment
- Parent issue marked dirty after comment ops
- Empty comment rejected
- Invalid issue_id rejected
- JSON output format correct

## Dependencies
- SQLite Storage Layer Core
- Model Types
- JSONL Export (for comments.jsonl)
- JSONL Import (for comments.jsonl)

## Rationale
Comments enable async collaboration on issues. They preserve discussion history and decisions, which is valuable when revisiting old issues or onboarding new team members.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:19:27.182569855Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:09.086093632Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:09.086093632Z","close_reason":"Superseded by beads_rust-trr (comments add/list only, classic behavior)"}
 {"id":"beads_rust-aeb","title":"list Command Implementation","description":"# list Command

## Purpose
Primary discovery interface with classic filter semantics and IssueWithCounts JSON output.

## CLI (core flags)
- Filters: `--status`, `--type`, `--assignee`, `--unassigned`, `--label`, `--label-any`, `--id`,
  `--priority`, `--priority-min`, `--priority-max`, `--created-after/before`, `--updated-after/before`, `--closed-after/before`.
- Text filters: `--title`, `--title-contains`, `--desc-contains`, `--notes-contains`.
- Scheduling: `--deferred`, `--defer-after/before`, `--due-after/before`, `--overdue`.
- Output: `--long`, `--pretty|--tree`, `--format` (template/dot/digraph), `--no-pager`.
- Other: `--ready` (open-only shortcut), `--all` (include closed), `--limit` (0=unlimited), `--sort`, `--reverse`.

## Default Semantics
- If no `--status` and no `--all`, **exclude closed**.
- `--ready` forces `status=open` only.
- Label AND/OR:
  - `--label` = AND (all labels).
  - `--label-any` = OR (any label).
- If **no labels provided**, apply `directory.labels` scoping from config.
- Default ordering: `priority ASC, created_at DESC`.
- `--sort` applies client-side sorting; `--reverse` flips order.
- Templates excluded unless `--include-templates` (if supported).
- `--limit 0` means unlimited; default 50 (agent mode 20).

## Output
- JSON: array of IssueWithCounts (Issue + dep counts).
- Text: compact, long, pretty/tree formats with status icons.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Filter semantics and ordering match bd.
- JSON output matches IssueWithCounts schema.

## Tests
- Filter combinations (labels AND/OR, status, dates).
- Default ordering and `--sort` behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:18:17.431316432Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T14:14:34.70542823Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T14:14:34.70542823Z","close_reason":"Implemented list command.  {"id":"beads_rust-ap0","title":"create Command Implementation","description":"# create Command

## Purpose
Create a new issue with classic bd flag semantics, validation, and dependency/label handling.

## CLI
```
br create <title> [OPTIONS]
br create --title <title> [OPTIONS]
```

## Core Flags
- `<title>`: Positional argument or `--title` (must match if both provided).
- `--type <type>`: Issue type (task, bug, feature, epic, chore, docs, question). Default: task.
- `--priority <0-4|P0-P4>`: Priority level. Default: 2 (medium).
- `--description <text>`: Issue description body.
- `--body <text>`: Alias for `--description`.
- `--body-file <path>`: Read description from file.
- `--design <text>`: Design notes section.
- `--acceptance <text>`: Acceptance criteria section.
- `--notes <text>`: Additional notes section.
- `--assignee <name>`: Assign to user.
- `--owner <name>`: Set owner.
- `--estimate <minutes>`: Time estimate in minutes.
- `--labels <label,...>`: Comma-separated labels (can repeat flag).
- `--deps <type:id|id>`: Add dependencies (default type: `blocks`).
- `--parent <id>`: Create as child of parent issue (creates parent-child dependency).
- `--external-ref <ref>`: External reference (e.g., JIRA-123).
- `--due <date>`: Due date (natural time parsing).
- `--defer <date>`: Defer until date (natural time parsing).
- `--id <id>`: Explicit ID (prefix validated unless `--force`).
- `--force`: Skip prefix validation for explicit ID.
- `--silent`: Output ID only (no other messages).
- `--dry-run`: Preview without creating.
- `--json`: JSON output.

## Behavior
1. **Title validation**: Length 1-500 characters. Error if empty or too long.
2. **Priority validation**: Must be 0-4 or P0-P4. Error if invalid.
3. **Description requirement**: If `create.require-description` config is set, warn/error if missing.
4. **Title prefix warning**: If title starts with "test", warn unless `--silent`.
5. **ID generation**:
   - If `--id` provided, validate prefix matches `issue_prefix` or `allowed_prefixes` (unless `--force`).
   - Otherwise, generate base36 adaptive-length hash ID.
6. **Dependencies**: Parse `--deps` values. Format: `type:id` or just `id` (default: `blocks`).
   - Invalid dependency types: warn and skip.
   - Validate target IDs exist (unless external).
7. **Labels**: Add all specified labels after creation.
8. **Parent handling**: If `--parent`, create `parent-child` dependency.
9. **Dirty marking**: Mark issue as dirty for export.
10. **Event emission**: Emit `created` event.
11. **Last-touched**: Set this issue as last-touched.

## Output

### JSON
```json
{
  "id": "bd-abc12",
  "title": "Implement feature X",
  "status": "open",
  "priority": 2,
  "issue_type": "feature",
  "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z",
  "created_by": "alice"
}
```

### Text Output

```

### Dry-Run Mode

Would create issue:

  Type: feature
  Priority: P2
  Labels: backend, api
  Dependencies: bd-xyz89 (blocks)
```

## Error Handling
- **EmptyTitle**: Title is empty → error with message.
- **TitleTooLong**: Title exceeds 500 chars → error with message.
- **InvalidPriority**: Priority not in valid range → error with suggestion.
- **InvalidType**: Issue type not recognized → error with valid types list.
- **InvalidDependencyTarget**: Dependency target does not exist → warning (non-fatal).
- **InvalidDependencyType**: Dependency type not recognized → warning with valid types.
- **PrefixMismatch**: Explicit ID prefix does not match config → error (unless `--force`).
- **IdCollision**: Explicit ID already exists → error.

 {"id":"beads_rust-u23","title":"reopen Command Implementation","description":"# reopen Command

## Purpose
Explicitly reopen closed issues (distinct from update --status open), matching bd behavior with optional comment insertion.

## CLI
```
br reopen <id...> [--reason <text>]
```

## Flags
- `<id...>`: One or more issue IDs (partial resolution). If none, uses last-touched.
- `--reason <text>`: Reason for reopening, stored as a comment on the issue.

## Behavior
1. Resolve issue ID(s) via partial matching.
2. For each issue:
   - Validate issue is currently closed; warn if already open.
   - Set `status=open`.
   - Clear `closed_at` field (set to null).
   - If `--reason` provided, add a comment: "Reopened: <reason>".
   - Emit `reopened` event to event log.
   - Mark issue as dirty for export.
3. Rebuild blocked cache (reopened issues may become blockers).

## Output

### JSON
Array of reopened Issue objects:
```json
[
  {
    "id": "bd-abc12",
    "title": "Implement feature",
    "status": "open",
    "closed_at": null
  }
]
```

### Text Output
```
✓ Reopened bd-abc12: Implement feature
  Reason: Found additional edge case
```

## Error Handling
- **IssueNotFound**: If ID doesnt resolve → error with suggestions.
- **AmbiguousId**: If ID resolves to multiple → error with candidate list.
- **AlreadyOpen**: Warning (not error) if issue already open.

## Logging
```rust
tracing::info!(id = %issue.id, "Reopening issue");
tracing::debug!(previous_status = ?old_status, "Issue was previously {:?}", old_status);
tracing::info!(id = %issue.id, reason = ?reason, "Issue reopened");
if let Some(reason) = reason {
    tracing::debug!(id = %issue.id, "Adding reopen comment");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Comment inserted when reason provided.
- closed_at is cleared (set to null).
- JSON shape matches bd.
- Multiple IDs work correctly.

    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Already open");
    
    // Issue is already open, reopen should warn
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["reopen", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stderr(predicate::str::contains("already open").or(predicate::str::is_empty()));
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_nonexistent_fails() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");

#[test]
fn test_reopen_multiple_issues() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let id1 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 1");
    let id2 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 2");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["close", &id1, &id2])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["reopen", &id1, &id2])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Verify both reopened
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["list", "--status", "open", "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(json.as_array().unwrap().len(), 2);
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_affects_blocked_cache() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let blocker = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocker");
    let blocked = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocked");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["dep", "add", &blocked, &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Close blocker, blocked should become ready
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["close", &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .arg("ready")
        .assert()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Blocked"));
    
    // Reopen blocker, blocked should become blocked again
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["reopen", &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .arg("blocked")
        .assert()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Blocked"));
}

 {"id":"beads_rust-u5s","title":"orphans Command (git-commit reference scan)","description":"# orphans Command (git commit reference scan)

## Purpose
Identify open/in-progress issues referenced in git commits but not yet closed. This is a read-only view used to catch missing close operations.

## Behavior (classic)
- Runs `git log --oneline --all` and extracts IDs like `(bd-abc123)`.
- Prefix is read from DB config `issue_prefix` (fallback default `bd`).
- Supports hierarchical IDs (e.g., `bd-abc.1`).
- For each issue ID, keep only the **latest (most recent)** commit reference.
- Only return issues whose status is `open` or `in_progress`.
- If not a git repo, missing `.beads/`, or missing DB: return empty list (no error).

## CLI
```
br orphans [--details] [--fix]
```
- `--details`: include latest commit hash + message in human output.
- `--fix`: **interactive** close flow (no JSON batch). Should prompt for confirmation
  then run `br close <id> --reason Implemented` per issue.

```json
[
  {
    "issue_id": "bd-abc",
    "title": "Fix edge-case",
    "status": "in_progress",
    "latest_commit": "deadbeef",
    "latest_commit_message": "Fix edge-case for parser"
  }
]
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Matches commit parsing and latest-commit selection rules.
- Returns empty list (not error) when git/DB not available.
- JSON shape matches bd.

## Tests
- Fixture repo with commits referencing issue IDs; verify latest commit selection.

 {"id":"beads_rust-uin","title":"prime Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br prime` command for displaying context about the current beads state. This is designed for AI agents to quickly understand the project's issue state after context loss (e.g., after compaction or starting a new session).

## CLI Interface
```
br prime [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --full                      Include all context (ready + in_progress + recent)
  --ready-only                Only show ready issues
  --limit <N>                 Limit issues per category (default: 10)
  --json                      Output as JSON
  --robot                     Machine-readable output
```

## Output Structure

The prime command outputs a comprehensive snapshot for agent context recovery:

```
# Beads Context Recovery
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## Project: beads_rust
Prefix: beads_rust-
Schema: v3
Issues: 156 total (42 open, 8 in_progress, 106 closed)

## In Progress (8)
Your active work:

[P0] beads_rust-abc123  Fix critical auth bug
     Assignee: alice | Updated: 2 hours ago
     Blocked by: (none - ready to work)

[P1] beads_rust-def456  Implement user dashboard
     Assignee: bob | Updated: 1 day ago
     Blocked by: beads_rust-xyz789 (Config system)

## Ready to Work (top 10)
Unblocked issues by priority:

[P0] beads_rust-ghi789  Security patch for XSS
[P1] beads_rust-jkl012  Add pagination to list API
[P1] beads_rust-mno345  Refactor error handling
...

## Recently Updated (top 5)
Latest activity:

[closed] beads_rust-pqr678  Setup CI/CD pipeline
         Closed 3 hours ago by alice

[updated] beads_rust-stu901  Database migration scripts
          Updated 5 hours ago (status: open → in_progress)

## Critical Blockers
Issues blocking the most work:

1. beads_rust-xyz789 (Config system) - blocks 5 issues
2. beads_rust-vwx234 (Auth middleware) - blocks 3 issues

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Commands: br ready | br show <id> | br update <id> --status=in_progress
```

### Implementation
```rust
fn prime(&self, opts: PrimeOptions) -> Result<PrimeContext> {
    let stats = self.get_stats()?;
    let in_progress = self.list_issues(ListQuery { 
        status: Some(Status::InProgress),
        limit: opts.limit,
        ..Default::default()
    })?;
    let ready = self.get_ready_issues(ReadyFilters {
        limit: opts.limit,
        ..Default::default()
    })?;
    let recent = self.get_recent_activity(opts.limit)?;
    let blockers = self.find_critical_blockers(5)?;
    
    Ok(PrimeContext {
        stats,
        in_progress,
        ready,
        recent,
        blockers,
    })
}
```

## JSON Output
```json
{
  "project": "beads_rust",
  "stats": {
    "total": 156,
    "open": 42,
    "in_progress": 8,
    "closed": 106
  },
  "in_progress": [...],
  "ready": [...],
  "recent": [...],
  "critical_blockers": [...]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Show project summary (name, prefix, counts)
- [ ] List in-progress issues
- [ ] List ready-to-work issues
- [ ] Show recent activity
- [ ] Identify critical blockers
- [ ] Human-readable and JSON output
- [ ] Configurable limits
- [ ] Fast execution (< 100ms)

## Dependencies
- Requires stats Command infrastructure
- Requires ready Command infrastructure
- Requires blocked Command infrastructure

## Rationale
AI agents lose context frequently due to context window limits. The prime command provides everything an agent needs to resume work effectively. It answers: "What was I working on? What can I work on? What's blocking progress?"
